entry.title: Why Stock Fields Return Zero in Custom CDS Views on MARC - and How to Resolve It
entry.content:
Why Stock Fields Return Zero in Custom CDS Views on MARC - and How to Resolve It

I was building a custom CDS view on the MARC table (Plant Data for Material) to extract data into SAP Datasphere using Change Data Capture (CDC) for delta loads.

After activating the view, I checked the data and found something odd. The field TRAME (Stock in Transit) had a proper value in the MARC table, but in my CDS view the same field was completely empty.

To give a concrete example, for one material and plant the MARC table showed TRAME = 50.000, but my CDS view showed TRAME = 0.000 for the same key. All the master data fields were perfectly fine. Only the stock field was wrong.

That single clue — master data correct, stock field zero — is what pointed me to the real cause.


Where the data actually lives in S/4HANA

In the older SAP ECC system, stock quantities were physically saved in many tables, and the same figure was often repeated in several places. This created redundancy and slowed things down.

In S/4HANA, SAP redesigned this part of the system with the New Simplified Data Model (NSDM). The idea is straightforward: all stock movements are written into a single table called MATDOC, and the stock figures are calculated when you ask for them instead of being stored everywhere.

Because of this change, MARC became a so-called hybrid table. It still physically stores the material master and planning data, but the stock quantity fields are no longer filled in the table itself. Fields like TRAME, UMLMC, GLGMG and BWESB are now worked out on the fly from MATDOC whenever they are read.

So in S/4HANA, the TRAME column inside the physical MARC table is basically empty. The real stock-in-transit figure is calculated from MATDOC only at the moment it is needed.

Why SE16 showed the value but my CDS did not

This is the most important point of the whole story. SE16 and my CDS view were reading the same table through two completely different paths.

When you open MARC in SE16, the read goes through the normal application layer. SAP automatically redirects that read to a proxy object behind MARC, which performs the MATDOC calculation and returns the correct TRAME value. That is why SE16 happily showed 50.000.

My CDS view, on the other hand, was written as select from marc. A CDS view runs straight on the database and reads the physical MARC table directly. It never passes through the redirect, so it picked up the empty physical column and returned 0.000.

In one sentence:

SE16 reads MARC through the proxy and gets the calculated value. A CDS that selects directly from MARC reads the raw table and gets nothing. That difference in the read path was the entire reason the data "did not come."


My first fix — and why it failed

My first idea was simple. If the proxy returns the correct value, why not read from the proxy instead of the table? So I changed the source of my view to the proxy object:

Before (TRAME comes as zero):

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_MARC_DEX'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Plant Data for Material'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@analytics.dataExtraction:{enabled: true,

delta.changeDataCapture:{mapping:[

{table:'marc',role:#MAIN,

viewElement:['Matnr','Werks'],

tableElement:['matnr','werks']}]}}

define view ZCDS_MARC_DEX_CDC as select from marc

{

   key matnr as Matnr,

   key werks as Werks,

   pstat as Pstat,

   lvorm as Lvorm,

   bwtty as Bwtty,

   xchar as Xchar,

   trame as Trame,

... remaining MARC fields ...

After (TRAME correct and CDC activates):

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_MARC_DEX'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Plant Data for Material'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 

@analytics:{

dataExtraction.enabled: true,

dataExtraction.delta.changeDataCapture:{

mapping:[

   {

      table:'marc',

      role:#MAIN,

      viewElement:['Matnr','Werks'],

      tableElement:['matnr','werks']

   }]

}

}

define view ZCDS_MARC_DEX_CDC as select from nsdm_e_marc

{

   key matnr as Matnr,

   key werks as Werks,

   pstat as Pstat,

   lvorm as Lvorm,

   bwtty as Bwtty, 

   xchar as Xchar,

   trame as Trame,

... remaining MARC fields ...

The only real change is the data source: from marc to nsdm_e_marc. The CDC mapping still refers to the physical table marc


This is not only a MARC problem

The same behaviour happens with all NSDM stock tables in S/4HANA. If your CDS reads stock fields straight from any of these, you can get zeros:

  • MARC – plant stock fields (TRAME, UMLMC, GLGMG, BWESB)
  • MARD – storage location stock
  • MCHB – batch stock
  • MKOL – special / consignment stock
  • MSKA – sales order stock
  • MSKU – customer special stock
  • MSLB – stock with vendor (subcontracting)
  • MSPR, MSSA, MSSQ – project and sales order stock totals
  • MSTB, MSTE, MSTQ – stock in transit tables


A simple rule of thumb: if a table held stock quantities in ECC, in S/4HANA those quantities are now calculated, and a direct CDS read will return zero. Use the table's proxy instead.

How to find the correct proxy in your own system

You do not have to guess the name. For any table (example: MARC):

  1. Open the table in SE11.
  2. Choose menu Extras → Replacement Object to see the assigned proxy (for MARC it is NSDM_E_MARC).

Example – "MARC" Replacement Object

Conclusion

What first looked like a bug in my CDS view turned out to be normal S/4HANA behaviour. Under the New Simplified Data Model, stock quantity fields are no longer stored in tables like MARC — they are calculated from MATDOC only when they are read. SE16 shows the correct value because it goes through the replacement object, while a CDS view that reads the table directly picks up the empty physical column and returns zero.

The fix itself is small but important: source the CDS view from the replacement object (for MARC, NSDM_E_MARC) instead of the base table. After that one change, TRAME returned the correct value. And because this is standard NSDM behaviour, the same approach works for every stock table in the family — whenever a stock field comes back as zero, the first thing to check is whether you are reading the base table directly instead of its replacement object.

The bigger lesson I took away is to always confirm where a field's data actually lives before building a CDS view on top of a table. A quick look at SE11 → Extras → Replacement Object tells you whether a table is a hybrid table and which object you should really be reading from. That single check can save a lot of confusing "the value is there in SE16 but missing in my view" situations.

entry.title: CO Substitution during KO88 PM Order to set a Secondary (statistic) posting.
entry.content:
Hello Gurus
My customer ask to post a secondary posting to a Statistic Internal Order, when I settle the PM Order costs to a Cost-Center.
I manage the internal Order n° on PM-Order (ILOA-AUFNR), and I thought to use a CO subsitution made by user-exit on program ZRGGBS000 to set this secondary accounting. If i set manually the Order n° during the debug teh system works fine, a secondary statistic posting item is created. Unfortunally in structure COBL and  other input structures doesn't contain the PM Order in process, so is not possible to read the Statistic Order assigned in ILOA-AUFNR field. 
How to solve this issue to get the PM order n° in process ? is ti possible get this info by modify table GGB1, or read this info from the memory ? Thanks for your reply.
Claudio

entry.title: Re: Why Your SAP Analytics Cloud Model Choice Can Make or Break Your Reporting Performance
entry.content: I agree, Priya. I've had a similar experience. One additional point: it's easy to spend a lot of time refining a BW query, while an Import model allows you to build a quick POC and validate business requirements faster. Once the value is proven, you can still assess whether a Live model is worth the additional effort.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: від таблиць Excel до розумних дашбордів
entry.content:
Скільки годин на тиждень ваші колеги витрачають на зведення даних із різних систем в Excel, щоб потім зробити звіт для керівництва? Якщо відповідь «занадто багато» - самий час познайомитися з SAP Analytics Cloud.

Що таке SAP Analytics Cloud?

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - це хмарна платформа для бізнес-аналітики, планування та прогнозування від SAP. Вона об'єднує три функції, які раніше потребували окремих інструментів: BI-звітність, корпоративне планування та предиктивна аналітика - все в одному браузерному застосунку.

Три стовпи SAP Analytics Cloud

• Business Intelligence - інтерактивні дашборди, аналіз у режимі реального часу, Stories (розповіді на основі даних)

• Planning - бюджетування, прогнозування, сценарне планування. Замінює громіздкі Excel-моделі

• Predictive Analytics - вбудовані ML-моделі для прогнозів без участі data scientist

Підключення до SAP-систем

SAC природно інтегрується з усіма SAP-джерелами: S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, Datasphere, SuccessFactors. Дані оновлюються в реальному часі або за розкладом. Це означає, що фінансовий директор бачить актуальний баланс не раз на місяць після закриття, а щомиті.

💡 Порівняння: Power BI і Tableau - потужні інструменти для загальної аналітики. SAC виграє там, де потрібна глибока інтеграція з SAP-даними, вбудоване планування та знання бізнес-семантики SAP. Якщо у вас SAP - SAC є природним вибором.

SAC для нетехнічних користувачів

Одна з ключових переваг SAC - доступність для бізнес-аналітиків без технічних знань. Drag-and-drop побудова дашбордів, автоматичні підказки системи («Чому зріс цей показник?»), Smart Insights та Smart Predict дозволяють отримувати аналітику без допомоги IT-відділу.

З чого почати?

1. SAP пропонує безкоштовний Trial-акаунт SAC на 90 днів - спробуйте підключити тестові дані

2. Почніть з побудови одного дашборду для реального бізнес-завдання вашої компанії

3. Вивчіть SAP Learning Journey «SAP Analytics Cloud» на офіційній платформі

Компанії, що переходять від Excel-звітності до SAC, зазвичай вражені не лише зручністю, а й тим, скільки помилок і розбіжностей ховалося в їхніх старих таблицях. Дані з однієї системи - надійніші за дані з десяти файлів.

entry.title: Re: Configuring the replication of Cost Centers from SAP ERP to SuccessFactors Employee Central Usin

entry.title: Generate Barcode for each material master record item, to print it in the GR output form?
entry.content:
Hi Team, our client has raised a requirement wherein, they want to generate and activate Barcode for each material master record and print it in the GR output form. 

On checking on this,  I found that this can be achieved by using EAN and generating System Barcode in SE73, however, I am not clear on the process. 

It would be of immense help if anyone who has done this before or knows about this can share some insights. Thanks!

entry.title: Customer reference list in VA01 is shown but entries cannot be selected
entry.content:
The issue I am experiencing is that in app VA01, the list of historical entries in the customer reference field is displayed, but I cannot select any of the entries. As a result, I have to manually re-enter the values for each new customer order. I need support to resolve this problem.

(This entry was created using Joule in SAP4Me :-))

entry.title: Webinar: End-to-End Business Processes with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear lecturers, 

Join us on Tuesday, July 28, 2026 (10–11 AM ET | 16–17 CEST) for a live show-and-tell of three free resources designed to support teaching end-to-end business processes with SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • Online Course – A free 32-hour course available on SAP Learning Hub, student edition
  • Practice System – Access to a preconfigured live SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment
  • Academic Lesson – Ready-to-use teaching materials for the classroom

Live Q&A with experts included. We recommend exploring the resources before the event. Please register using your university email address.

Can't attend? The session will be recorded and published on SAP Community - subscribe to stay updated.

👉Register here: Webinar: End-to-End Business Processes with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Best regards,

SAP University Alliances program team
